title: Surfactant Deficiency & RDS: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Surfactant Deficiency & RDS
Identify key diagnostic features of Surfactant Deficiency & RDS
Apply clinical reasoning to Surfactant Deficiency & RDS management
Recognize common pitfalls in Surfactant Deficiency & RDS

sections.title: Overview of Surfactant Deficiency & RDS
sections.content:
**Surfactant Deficiency & RDS**

Type II pneumocyte development, surfactant composition, respiratory distress syndrome of newborn, lecithin-sphingomyelin ratio

**Key Points:**
• Surfactant produced by Type II pneumocytes starting ~24 weeks, adequate by ~35 weeks
• Surfactant = DPPC (dipalmitoylphosphatidylcholine) + surfactant proteins (SP-A, B, C, D)
• Reduces alveolar surface tension → prevents atelectasis

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Surfactant produced by Type II pneumocytes starting ~24 weeks, adequate by ~35 weeks
• Surfactant = DPPC (dipalmitoylphosphatidylcholine) + surfactant proteins (SP-A, B, C, D)

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Reduces alveolar surface tension → prevents atelectasis
• RDS: ground-glass opacities + air bronchograms on CXR
• L/S ratio ≥2.0 indicates lung maturity; Treatment: exogenous surfactant + antenatal corticosteroids

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Surfactant Deficiency & RDS:**

• Reduces alveolar surface tension → prevents atelectasis
• RDS: ground-glass opacities + air bronchograms on CXR
• L/S ratio ≥2.0 indicates lung maturity; Treatment: exogenous surfactant + antenatal corticosteroids

correct.reinforcement: Correct! Surfactant produced by Type II pneumocytes starting ~24 weeks, adequate by ~35 weeks.

coachDx.misconception: While typically self-resolving without treatment may seem plausible, it does not match the clinical scenario described.
coachDx.guidance: Review the key features that distinguish Surfactant produced by Type II pneumocytes starting ~24 weeks, adequate by ~35 w from Typically self-resolving without treatment.
coachDx.bridgeTo: The key distinction involves Surfactant produced by Type II pneumocytes starting ~24 weeks, adequate by ~35 weeks.
